channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Heavy Rain in Several Regions Over the Next Day
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a weather update forecasting heavy rainfall across several regions in the country over the next 24 hours. Starting from 9 pm on Tuesday, April 28, ...

item.title: Ex-Kwara APC Chairman Bolarinwa Declares 2027 Governorship Ambition, Promises Security and Development
item.description: Bashir Omolaja Bolarinwa, wey many people sabi as BOB and former chairman of APC for Kwara State, don officially declare say him wan contest for governor come 2027 election. Him make the announcement ...

item.title: My Legacy launches Green Frames initiative for women to document climate solutions through short films
item.description: In Tanzania, women are taking a leading role in fighting the effects of climate change, especially in areas where agriculture and natural resources are main sources of living. Prolonged droughts and d...

item.title: Trump welcomes King Charles III and Queen Camilla for state visit at the White House
item.description: President Donald Trump hosted a grand ceremonial welcome for King Charles III and Queen Camilla on the South Lawn of the White House during their state visit to the United States. The event, which inc...

item.title: Impact of prolonged sitting on health and practical ways to stay active at work
item.description: Regular physical activity is crucial for maintaining good health, mental well-being, and longevity. According to the World Health Organization, adults should engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate...

item.title: Coop Bank Tanzania Achieves Strong Growth and Boosts Financial Inclusion in First Year
item.description: Coop Bank Tanzania Plc is celebrating its one-year anniversary since being officially launched by President Samia Suluhu Hassan on April 28, 2025. During this short period, the bank has grown rapidly ...

item.title: UP Diliman Graduate Achieves Highest Score in April 2026 Electrical Engineers Licensure Exam
item.description: A graduate of the University of the Philippines-Diliman, John Veluz Labrador, emerged as the top scorer in the April 2026 registered electrical engineers licensure examination with a score of 94.25%, ...

item.title: Supreme Court hearing on Sabarimala case may have implications for multiple religions
item.description: The ongoing legal proceedings related to the Sabarimala temple case are now before a Supreme Court bench in India. While initially focused on the specific issue of entry restrictions at the Sabarimala...

item.title: Pemberton children’s train greeting tradition faces uncertainty as railway route may close
item.description: In Pemberton, British Columbia, a group of children has been welcoming visitors arriving on the Rocky Mountaineer train for over a decade. Known as the Welcome Committee, these kids, some as young as ...

item.title: Cape Town's Housing Contracts and Alleged Gang Influence: The Case for Madlanga Commission Oversight
item.description: This article investigates the alleged infiltration of gang-linked networks into Cape Town’s municipal systems, particularly housing and procurement. Central to the controversy are three alleged 'black...

item.title: Wife Schools and the Promotion of Traditional Roles in Conservative Christian Marriages
item.description: Wife School, an online course led by Tilly Dillehay, instructs women in conservative Christian communities on becoming submissive and supportive wives, presenting a model of domesticity aligned with c...

item.title: Cambodia to Repatriate 54 Pakistani Nationals Detained Without Legal Proceedings
item.description: On Tuesday, the Foreign Office spokesperson, Tahir Andrabi, confirmed that Cambodian authorities have agreed to facilitate the early repatriation of 54 Pakistani nationals who were detained in Siem Re...

item.title: Preview of Key Contenders at Wednesday's Kembla Grange Races
item.description: The Kembla Grange race meeting on Wednesday features promising three-year-olds from the Matthew Smith stable, including Beauty Swift and Glastonbury Girl. Beauty Swift, a son of Group 1 star Pierro, s...

item.title: Schedule and Guidelines for Release of Exit Poll Projections in West Bengal and Other States
item.description: With the second and final phase of West Bengal assembly elections scheduled on April 29, 2026, all eyes are on the timing and rules for exit poll projections. Voting in the first phase concluded on Ap...

item.title: Balancing Restitution and Redistribution in South African Land Reform
item.description: Land reform in South Africa remains one of the most complex post-1994 government initiatives, aimed at addressing historical injustices caused by colonialism and apartheid. The programme is structured...

item.title: Terra Industries Launches New Defense Technology to Support Nigerian Military Operations
item.description: Naija Gen Z startup, Terra Industries, don show new defense gadgets wey go help our soldiers handle insecurity better. Dem introduce interceptor drones, UAVs, mine-detection systems, and battlefield i...

item.title: Doctor Struck Off for Offering Unproven Cancer Treatments
item.description: Dr Mohsen Ali, a former UK-based doctor whose medical licence had been revoked in 2015, has been permanently struck off after running an unregistered clinic in Leicester and charging patients up to £1...

item.title: Canberra Start-up Liquid Instruments Secures $400 Million Valuation with Global Clients
item.description: Liquid Instruments, a Canberra-based start-up founded by scientists from the Australian National University, has achieved a valuation exceeding $400 million following a $70 million funding round. The ...

item.title: HSBC upgrades U.S. equities outlook amid strong earnings and easing geopolitical risks
item.description: HSBC has upgraded its stance on U.S. equities from 'neutral' to 'overweight', citing strong earnings momentum and a reduction in geopolitical risks as key factors. Nearly 30% of U.S. companies have re...

item.title: Kisii Family Forced to Bury Banana Stem After Morgue Denies Relative's Body Over Unpaid Bill
item.description: In Motonyoni, Nyariba Masaba constituency, a Kisii family faced a heartbreaking situation after a Nakuru mortuary withheld the body of their 68-year-old relative, Obadiah Mokua Obwogo, due to an outst...
